title: Fix Admin Reset (Anbarı Sıfırla) — wire up backend resetInventory function

requirements.text: Implement a resetInventory function in the Motoko backend actor that sets all product stock quantities to zero and persists the change in stable storage. The function must return a success result.

quotes: parolu yazib daxil ol basannan sora bele yazir 'Bu funksiya hal-hazırda mövcud deyil'

acceptanceCriteria: A resetInventory() function exists in backend/main.mo
Calling resetInventory sets every product's stock quantity to 0 in the stable products map
The function returns a success result (e.g., #ok)
The Candid interface exposes resetInventory so the frontend can call it

requirements.text: Update ResetInventory.tsx to remove the 'feature not implemented' placeholder and instead call the backend resetInventory function after the user passes the password gate and confirms the reset dialog. Show a success message in Azerbaijani on completion and an error message if the call fails.

quotes: Anbarı sıfırlamaq funksiyası bu versiyada dəstəklənmir

acceptanceCriteria: ResetInventory.tsx no longer shows the 'Bu funksiya hal-hazırda mövcud deyil' notice
After correct password entry and confirmation, the frontend calls the backend resetInventory function
A success message is displayed in Azerbaijani (e.g., 'Anbar uğurla sıfırlandı') upon a successful reset
An error message is shown in Azerbaijani if the backend call fails
After reset, stock levels reflect 0 for all products when viewed in the inventory screen

requirements.text: Add a useResetInventory mutation hook in useQueries.ts that calls the backend resetInventory function. On success, invalidate the stockLevels, salesHistory, and zReport queries so all screens reflect the updated (zeroed) state.
acceptanceCriteria: It calls the backend actor's resetInventory function as a mutation
On success, it invalidates stockLevels, salesHistory, and zReport queries
ResetInventory.tsx uses this hook to trigger the reset

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Password validation ('gulserxan') is handled client-side in the existing PasswordGate component — do not add password logic to the backend
nonGoals: Modifying any other page besides ResetInventory.tsx and useQueries.ts on the frontend
Resetting sales history records — only stock quantities should be zeroed
